# Test Fixtures

These `.npz` files are the ground truth for all Python validation tests.
They are generated by running the original MATLAB NLvib examples and saving outputs as
NumPy `.npz` archives.  The `analytical/` sub-directory holds fixtures that can be
computed without MATLAB (closed-form solutions used as secondary references).

---

## How to generate (requires MATLAB or Octave)

```bash
# 1. Download the original NLvib MATLAB source
bash tools/fetch_matlab_source.sh

# 2. Generate all fixtures via MATLAB
matlab -batch "run('tools/generate_fixtures.m')"
# --- or, with Octave ---
octave --no-gui tools/generate_fixtures.m

# 3. Inspect / summarise generated fixtures
python tools/generate_fixtures.py --list
python tools/generate_fixtures.py --inspect 01_Duffing
```

---

## NPZ Schema

Every MATLAB-generated fixture follows this canonical schema.
Arrays are stored as 1-D `float64` vectors of length `n_points` (one entry per
continuation step along the branch).

| Array key         | dtype   | Shape           | Description |
|-------------------|---------|-----------------|-------------|
| `omega`           | float64 | `(n_points,)`   | Excitation / natural frequency Ω (rad/s) |
| `amplitude`       | float64 | `(n_points,)`   | Response amplitude at DOF 0, harmonic 1 (m) |
| `phase`           | float64 | `(n_points,)`   | Phase angle of fundamental harmonic (rad) |
| `stability`       | bool    | `(n_points,)`   | `True` = stable, `False` = unstable branch |
| `tolerance`       | float64 | scalar          | Validation tolerance for this fixture (default `1e-6`) |

Additional arrays present in specific fixtures:

| Array key          | Present in                   | Description |
|--------------------|------------------------------|-------------|
| `omega_shoot`      | `01_Duffing`                 | Shooting-method frequency branch |
| `amplitude_shoot`  | `01_Duffing`                 | Shooting-method amplitude branch |
| `stability_shoot`  | `01_Duffing`                 | Stability from Floquet multipliers |
| `amplitude_dof2`   | `02–06` (multi-DOF)          | Amplitude at second DOF |
| `phase_dof2`       | `02–06` (multi-DOF)          | Phase at second DOF |
| `Q_harmonics`      | all                          | Full harmonic coefficient matrix `(n_dof, n_harm, n_points)` — raw continuation output |

The `Q_harmonics` array is the raw output from the continuation solver and supports
residual-level validation.  The scalar summary arrays (`omega`, `amplitude`, etc.)
are derived from `Q_harmonics` by the MATLAB fixture wrapper script.

---

## Fixture Catalogue

### MATLAB-generated fixtures  (`tools/generate_fixtures.m`)

| File | Task | NLvib example folder | System | Method |
|------|------|---------------------|--------|--------|
| `01_Duffing.npz` | T-15 | `01_Duffing` | 1-DOF Duffing oscillator | HB + Shooting overlay |
| `02_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring.npz` | T-16 | `02_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring` | 2-DOF cubic spring chain | HB |
| `03_twoDOFoscillator_unilateralSpring.npz` | T-17 | `03_twoDOFoscillator_unilateralSpring` | 2-DOF unilateral spring | HB |
| `04_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring_NM.npz` | T-18 | `04_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring_NM` | 2-DOF cubic spring | NMA backbone |
| `05_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_NM.npz` | T-18 | `05_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_NM` | 2-DOF tanh friction | NMA backbone |
| `06_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_FRF.npz` | T-18 | `05_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_NM` | 2-DOF tanh friction | HB FRF |
| `07_geometricNonlinearity.npz` | T-19 | `06_geometricNonlinearity` | Multi-DOF geometric NL | HB |
| `08_multiDOF_multiNL.npz` | T-20 | `06_multiDOFoscillator` | Multi-DOF, multiple NL elements | HB |
| `09_beam_tanhFriction.npz` | T-21 | `07_beam_tanhFriction` | FE Euler-Bernoulli beam + tanh friction | HB |
| `10_beam_cubicSpring_NM.npz` | T-22 | `08_beam_cubicSpring_NM` | FE beam + cubic spring | NMA backbone |

### Analytical fixtures (no MATLAB required)

| File | Description | Equation |
|------|-------------|----------|
| `analytical/duffing_backbone.npz` | Duffing oscillator backbone curve | ω² = ω₀² + (3/4) k₃ A²  (ω₀=1, k₃=1) |

Arrays in `analytical/duffing_backbone.npz`:

| Key | Shape | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `amplitude` | `(200,)` | Modal amplitude A ∈ [0.01, 2.0], 200 log-spaced points |
| `omega`     | `(200,)` | Backbone natural frequency ω (rad/s) |

---

## MATLAB Version Record

Fill in when fixtures are regenerated:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| MATLAB version | _(e.g., R2023b)_ |
| Octave version (alternative) | _(e.g., 8.4.0)_ |
| NLvib branch | `main` (NLvib-Basic) |
| NLvib commit SHA | _(fill in)_ |
| Generation script | `tools/generate_fixtures.m` |
| Generated on | _(YYYY-MM-DD)_ |

---

## Validation Protocol (QA Agent)

The QA agent compares Python output against these fixtures using:

```python
rel_error = abs(python_result - matlab_result) / (abs(matlab_result) + 1e-12)
assert rel_error.max() <= fixture["tolerance"]
```

The default `tolerance` stored in each fixture is `1e-6`.
Examples with non-smooth nonlinearities (unilateral spring, tanh friction at low
smoothing parameter) may use a relaxed tolerance of `1e-4`; this is recorded in the
fixture's `tolerance` scalar so the comparison is always self-documented.

---

## Generation Status

| Fixture | Generated | MATLAB version | Notes |
|---------|-----------|----------------|-------|
| `01_Duffing.npz` | — | — | Primary smoke test; HB + Shooting |
| `02_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring.npz` | — | — | |
| `03_twoDOFoscillator_unilateralSpring.npz` | — | — | Expect relaxed tol 1e-4 |
| `04_twoDOFoscillator_cubicSpring_NM.npz` | — | — | Backbone curve |
| `05_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_NM.npz` | — | — | Backbone curve |
| `06_twoDOFoscillator_tanhDryFriction_FRF.npz` | — | — | FRF at multiple damping levels |
| `07_geometricNonlinearity.npz` | — | — | |
| `08_multiDOF_multiNL.npz` | — | — | |
| `09_beam_tanhFriction.npz` | — | — | Large system; may take >10 min |
| `10_beam_cubicSpring_NM.npz` | — | — | NMA; slow to generate |
| `analytical/duffing_backbone.npz` | YES | N/A | Closed-form; always present |

> **Note — Example 06 fixtures (`08_multiDOF_multiNL.npz`)**: when regenerating
> this fixture, ensure the Python side uses the `elastic_dry_friction` element
> (from `nlvib.nonlinearities.elements`) rather than `tanh_dry_friction`.
> The MATLAB example `06_multiDOFoscillator` uses a Jenkins slider
> (`elasticDryFriction`), so only the hysteretic element produces a valid
> comparison.  Using `tanh_dry_friction` as a proxy will cause the fixture
> validation to fail at the default `1e-6` tolerance.
